mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate transmits through a lower discount rate and easier financial conditions, supporting equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy rate is near zero and unchanged.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher than unconditional baseline when FEDFUNDS is stable at very low levels, or if the sign flips negative in a rolling 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation acceleration above prior trend forces higher expected policy rates and tighter real discount rates, which compresses equity multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of CPI inflation-acceleration triggers, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment signals improving household income and demand resilience, which supports revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not positive after unemployment declines, or if triggered cases underperform the unconditional distribution across any 2-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in retail sales reflects stronger nominal demand and better near-term corporate revenue growth, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month when sales momentum is positive versus the prior quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in more than one contiguous 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger current output and inventory restocking, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if SPX underperforms after positive INDPRO inflections, or if the signal loses predictive value such that Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling interval.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rebound in housing starts and payrolls signals synchronized household demand and labor income growth, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because the effect is stronger when both series improve together than when either improves alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined HOUST-plus-PAYEMS condition does not outperform each series separately, or if triggered 60-day SPX returns are not above unconditional returns in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When payrolls rise but unemployment also rises, job creation is not absorbing labor supply and the labor market is deteriorating beneath headline growth, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases while UNRATE rises by at least 0.1 percentage points in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration condition does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is indistinguishable from zero over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, an elevated federal funds rate combined with accelerating CPI inflation creates a higher real-rate shock and multiple compression, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate and rising-inflation condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of tightening episodes defined by rising real-rate pressure.
